preamble: The shift from the bilabial nasal [m] to the alveolar nasal [n] is a common form of place assimilation, particularly in word-final positions. In monosyllabic words and inflectional endings, many languages prefer the more neutral alveolar point of articulation.

examples.note: Monosyllabic final m shifted to n.

languageExamples.language: Old High German to Middle High German

examples.note: Inflectional m shifted to n in unstressed syllables.

sources: Penny: A History of the Spanish Language
